title: eriogonum abramsii subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum abramsii subsp. typicum is a rare and unique subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae), commonly known as the Abrams' buckwheat or Abrams' desert buckwheat. This desert shrub is native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the Mojave and Sonoran Deserts. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Eriogonum abramsii subsp. typicum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, although its commercial availability is limited due to its rarity.
